title: Run Rootless Error:  cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
I am unable to run podman as rootless. 
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.podman run hello-world


**Describe the results you received:**

Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world...ERRO[0000] Error pulling image ref //registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world:latest: Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/hello-world: name unknown: Repo not found 
Failed
Trying to pull docker.io/library/hello-world...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 1b930d010525 done
Copying config fce289e99e done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: container create failed: cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
: internal libpod error

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect the hello-world container to be running
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
-bash-4.2$ podman --version
podman version 1.3.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.8
  podman version: 1.3.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8.2
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.3.2-1.git14fdcd0.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.14.0-dev, commit: 4780496c1f6949e21c807e17b57c183e9424c90a-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.6"
  MemFree: 131399319552
  MemTotal: 134958702592
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-59.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 17179865088
  SwapTotal: 17179865088
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 40
  hostname: bluejgpu6
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.21.3.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 2h 10m 54.79s (Approximately 0.08 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/xxx/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /tmp/2287
  VolumePath: /home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
physical host.

Static hostname: xxxx
         Icon name: computer-server
           Chassis: server
        Machine ID: 6af2c3ebedfc4df4bcdb9cc0d6789cdb
           Boot ID: 6da22ea95bd947ffbed868dd42e04b93
  Operating System: Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server 7.6 (Maipo)
       CPE OS Name: cpe:/o:redhat:enterprise_linux:7.6:GA:server
            Kernel: Linux 3.10.0-957.21.3.el7.x86_64
      Architecture: x86-64

-bash-4.2$ runc --version
runc version spec: 1.0.0

comments.body: I believe this is usually caused by a too-old `runc` version. RHEL/CentOS 7.7 should have a new enough version to include a fix for this.

yes, this is caused by runc, it raises this error even if there are enough IDs mapped inside the namespace.

Could you try with an updated runc?

Please re-open the issue if it still doesn't work and you believe the problem is Podman
@giuseppe can you help me on how to get newer  `runc` on RHEL 7.6, please?
@asm582 I'd just build it from upstream: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc

Thank you.

On Thu, Jul 25, 2019 at 3:45 AM Giuseppe Scrivano <notifications@github.com>
